# Quote templates

Quote templates are for **manual quote takeoff** jobs (not digital takeoff). They save time when you quote specific jobs a lot and do not want to manually add components every time.

For example, you might quote a lot of standard roofs that normally contain many of the same components - corrugate longrun with the same fixings, underlay, hips, valleys, ridges, barges, and delivery fee. Rather than create a new quote and manually add all those components, you save a template once and then load it from the dropdown of your templates when you start a new quote. All those components auto-populate. From there, you add the specific areas, lengths, and quantities to each component as you normally would.

Templates are designed to save you time. Every little bit helps.

## Where to find it

**Templates > Quote templates**.

## When to use a template

- You quote a lot of similar jobs (e.g. standard corrugate re-roofs).
- You want a faster starting point than a blank quote.
- You want consistency across jobs.

Skip templates if every job is bespoke or if you build quotes with digital takeoff (templates do not apply there).
